content:
@Gary Guo can we do pointer projection in dma address space? I have this line:

            let command_desc_base_addr =
                thing_list.dma_handle() + (tag * core::mem::size_of::<Thing>()) as dma::DmaAddress;

and I thought I should be able to do something like:

            let command_desc_base_addr = kernel::ptr::project!(thing_list.dma_handle(), [tag]?)

But obviously DmaAddress is not a pointer and it does not work. But it would be nice if it could work, I think it is easier to read.
You can project Coherent to get a CoherentView and get dma_handle() from it
Ha, I read that patch at v2 :sweat_smile: Thanks.
Andreas Hindborg has marked this topic as resolved.
@Gary Guo do you happen to have a tree of this series rebased on master or rust-next?
It needs zerocopy compatibility treatment I think.

It build like so:

diff --git a/drivers/pwm/pwm_th1520.rs b/drivers/pwm/pwm_th1520.rs
index 3e3fa51ccef91..46e0d92afc4e8 100644
--- a/drivers/pwm/pwm_th1520.rs
+++ b/drivers/pwm/pwm_th1520.rs
@@ -20,7 +20,6 @@
 //!   this method is not used in this driver.
 //!

-use core::ops::Deref;
 use kernel::{
     clk::Clk,
     device::{Bound, Core, Device},
@@ -214,7 +213,7 @@ fn read_waveform(
         let data = chip.drvdata();
         let hwpwm = pwm.hwpwm();
         let iomem_accessor = data.iomem.access(parent_dev)?;
-        let iomap = iomem_accessor.deref();
+        let iomap = iomem_accessor;

         let ctrl = iomap.try_read32(th1520_pwm_ctrl(hwpwm))?;
         let period_cycles = iomap.try_read32(th1520_pwm_per(hwpwm))?;
@@ -249,7 +248,7 @@ fn write_waveform(
         let data = chip.drvdata();
         let hwpwm = pwm.hwpwm();
         let iomem_accessor = data.iomem.access(parent_dev)?;
-        let iomap = iomem_accessor.deref();
+        let iomap = iomem_accessor;
         let duty_cycles = iomap.try_read32(th1520_pwm_fp(hwpwm))?;
         let was_enabled = duty_cycles != 0;

diff --git a/rust/kernel/io.rs b/rust/kernel/io.rs
index 5cd4756cf2371..d9abd93eec1c8 100644
--- a/rust/kernel/io.rs
+++ b/rust/kernel/io.rs
@@ -98,6 +98,7 @@ fn size(p: *const Self) -> usize {
 // `repr(align(4))`).
 unsafe impl<const SIZE: usize> IntoBytes for Region<SIZE> {
     #[inline]
+    #[allow(dead_code)]
     fn only_derive_is_allowed_to_implement_this_trait() {}
 }

@@ -1457,7 +1458,9 @@ fn as_view(self) -> <Self::Backend as IoBackend>::View<'a, Self::Target> {
 ///
 /// [`include/linux/iosys-map.h`](srctree/include/linux/iosys-map.h)
 pub enum IoSysMap<'a, T: ?Sized> {
+    /// TODO
     Io(Mmio<'a, T>),
+    /// TODO
     Sys(SysMem<'a, T>),
 }
This is known and I'm sending out a new version later today
Oh the PWM issue is not known (I didn't realize that my config has it disabled). Will add a patch to fix this. It shouldn't be using .deref() anyway.
I had COMPILE_TEST enabled to build Tyr, but I didn't realize that the PWM_TH1520 needs it as well
